commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2a0b01f7ea322915993722b23c227732ad9160c8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds memory-core SQLite index identity validation and gating for provider/model/settings mismatches, CLI warnings, search/sync retry changes, focused tests, and a scratch implementation-plan markdown file.

PR surface: Source +250, Tests +689, Docs +73. Total +1012 across 21 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR finding from source inspection: a mismatched index makes `manager.search()` return `[]`, the tool only skips forced sync, and the added test asserts a normal empty result. I did not run a live Docker/Ollama cutover.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR with a concrete P1 tool-payload defect plus provider/session upgrade semantics that need author and maintainer resolution before merge.

target.title: fix(memory): validate memory index identity
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (`osolmaz`).

AI-assisted contribution.

## Summary

Changing the memory embedding provider can leave an old SQLite vector index behind.
This change stops OpenClaw from trusting that index when its saved provider/model/settings identity no longer matches the current memory provider.
It does this without changing the SQLite schema, config, commands, or plugin surface, and without silently doing an expensive rebuild on startup or first search.

Fixes #83333.

## What Changed

The builtin memory SQLite DB is treated as one provider-specific index.
If the stored index identity is valid, search works normally.
If the identity is missing or mismatched, memory marks the index dirty, pauses vector search, and tells the user how to repair it with the existing indexing flow.

- Added a shared DB-level index identity check for provider, model, provider key, sources, scope, chunking, tokenizer, and vector metadata.
- Made status/search/sync respect that identity before trusting persisted rows.
- Stopped normal startup, watch sync, targeted session sync, and `memory_search` zero-hit retry from rebuilding a mismatched index automatically.
- Kept explicit repair on the existing paths: `openclaw memory status --index` and `openclaw memory index --force`.
- Added CLI warnings so users see why vector search is paused and how to rebuild.
- Added focused SQLite/Vitest coverage for provider/model cutover, missing identity metadata, provider alias identity, fallback repair, targeted session sync, and the `memory_search` retry path.

## What This Avoids

The expensive part is re-embedding an existing memory corpus.
This change avoids doing that automatically because a provider cutover could otherwise surprise users with cost, latency, or local model work.

- No SQLite data-model or migration change.
- No new config value.
- No new command.
- No plugin API or plugin surface change.
- No automatic full reindex on startup, status, watch reload, normal search, or first agent `memory_search` retry.

## Repair Behavior

Repair is explicit.
Users rebuild the paused index with the existing indexing commands when they are ready to pay the provider/local-model cost.

- `openclaw memory status --index`
- `openclaw memory index --force`

Until then, OpenClaw does not mix vectors from different embedding providers or models.

## Testing

The focused memory tests pass locally, and the PR is mergeable against current `main`.
The GitHub checks that ran after marking ready passed; broader CI/CodeQL lanes may still run or be skipped by path gating.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-targeted-sync.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts` - passed, 6 files / 146 tests.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `pnpm format:check extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory-tool-manager-mock.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts` - passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test --pretty false` - passed.
- `codex review --base main` - no actionable correctness issues.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Memory no longer treats a persisted vector index as trustworthy after the active embedding provider/model/settings no longer match the identity saved with that index.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout with Node/Vitest, SQLite memory index fixtures, and mocked embedding providers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-targeted-sync.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: Tests cover building an index under one provider/model, reopening the same DB under another provider/model, marking the index dirty, pausing search, preventing automatic tool-triggered reindex, and repairing through explicit indexing.

Observed result after fix: The focused suite passed, 6 files / 146 tests.

What was not tested: No live Docker/Ollama production cutover was run. The proof is source review plus focused local SQLite/Vitest coverage.

## Risks

The intentional behavior change is that provider/model cutover can make vector memory search unavailable until the user explicitly rebuilds the index.
That is safer than mixing OpenAI/Ollama vectors or silently spending provider/local compute to rebuild everything.

- Users may see fewer memory results until they run the existing rebuild flow.
- Provider alias/providerKey and session-memory cutovers still need maintainer acceptance as upgrade behavior.
- The current fix does not gradually repair individual rows because the DB does not store row-level provider identity, and this PR intentionally avoids adding that data model.
target.author: osolmaz

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
